init.template: Ask the user which locale they want for this project (pt for Portuguese, es for Spanish, or en for English). Then initialize the `.opencode/` project configuration in the current working directory by running the init script with the chosen locale. After init completes, verify `.opencode/locale` contains the chosen language. Then review the generated files.
init.description: Initialize opencode project config with repo context and locale

ocf:plan-feature.template: Break down the feature into steps, identify impacted layers, and register risks and planned work in `.opencode/known_issues.md`.

ocf:promote.template: Ask the user how many Senior Reviewers should review the work after development (default 1). Do NOT read reviewer count from opencode.json. Store the count as `- Reviewers: <n>` in the issue entry in `known_issues.md`. Then promote a tracked backlog item inside `known_issues.md`. Change `Status` from `backlog` or `ready` to `open` and reset `Remote` to `-`. Then ask the user: "Criar issue remota agora? (s/N)". If yes, run the create_issue script to create the remote issue, update `Remote: #<id>` and `Status: in-progress`, and switch to the generated branch. If no, keep status as `open` — the developer MUST NOT start development without a remote issue.
ocf:promote.description: Promote backlog item to open and create remote issue

ocf:review-branch.template: Ask the user how many Senior Reviewers should perform this review (default 1). Do NOT read reviewer count from opencode.json. Read `- Reviewers:` from the issue entry in `known_issues.md` instead. Then run a full PR/MR-style review: analyze `git diff`, use that many Senior Engineer reviewers, identify Critical and Major issues only, validate tests and docs, and register new issues.

ocf:develop.template:
Ask the user which issue ID to develop (or detect from current branch name matching `issue-<id>-<slug>`). Read the issue entry from `known_issues.md`. Check the Status:
- If `backlog` or `ready`: run the promote flow first — ask base branch, reviewer count, create remote issue, then proceed.
- If `open` with Remote `-`: refuse — tell the user to create the remote first via `ocf:promote` or `ocf:sync-issues`.
- If `open` with Remote set or `in-progress`: proceed.
- If `in-review`, `in-qa`, `in-publish`, or `resolved`: refuse — issue is past development phase.
If proceeding, verify the current git branch matches `issue-<id>-<slug>`; if not, checkout the correct branch. Then invoke the Developer agent with the full issue context (title, description, business rules, acceptance criteria, location, branch) to implement the feature or fix.
ocf:develop.description: Start or resume development on an issue, promoting first if needed

ocf:scan-issues.template: Run a deep analysis of the codebase to detect new issues (security, concurrency, architecture, reliability). Create or update `.opencode/known_issues.md`. Follow the issue entry format from `standards/issues.md`.
ocf:scan-issues.description: Deep codebase analysis and issue detection

ocf:commit.template: Create an atomic semantic commit following `standards/commits.md`. Use format `<type>(<scope>): <imperative description>` with `Issue: #<id>` and `Status:` trailers. Ask for type/scope/description/issue-id. Detect the project known_issues.md, construct the commit, run `git commit`, and update known_issues.md status based on trailers. Split if the change covers multiple concerns.

ocf:sync-issues.template: Scan known_issues.md entries with Remote: references, fetch remote issue state via gh/glab, sync status (close remote if local resolved, reopen if mismatch), create remote issues for open entries without remote, and report sync results.
ocf:sync-issues.description: Sync known_issues with remote issue tracker

ocf:archive-issue.template: Move a resolved issue from known_issues.md to resolved_issues.md in compact format. The issue must be Status: resolved. Use close_issue.sh which handles both closing and archiving in one step.
ocf:archive-issue.description: Archive resolved issue to compact format

ocf:maintain.template: Run full maintenance on known_issues.md and standards/prioritization.md. First run `$SCRIPTS_DIR/maintain.sh` to detect stale issues (local in-progress but remote closed) and status mismatches. Then for each stale issue, run `close_issue.sh <id>` to archive. Reindex remaining issues. Report what was archived/updated.

ocf:backup.template: Run `$SCRIPTS_DIR/backup.sh` with the provided arguments. Ask the user for: 1) source directory, 2) backup name (default dev_backup), 3) whether to create a .zip. Then execute the backup. After completion, report the backup path, size, and exclusions applied.
ocf:backup.description: Create intelligent timestamped backup excluding junk
